title: Apollonius's theorem (median length theorem)
informal_statement: For a triangle with vertices A, B, C and M the midpoint of side BC: AB^2 + AC^2 = 2*AM^2 + 2*BM^2 (Apollonius's theorem, a.k.a. the median length theorem).
informal_proof_idea: Substitute M = ((xB+xC)/2, (yB+yC)/2) and expand both sides as polynomials in the six coordinates; the identity is a direct consequence of the parallelogram law and closes by `ring`.
formal_statement_pp: theorem apollonius_median (xA yA xB yB xC yC : ℝ) : (xA - xB) ^ 2 + (yA - yB) ^ 2 + ((xA - xC) ^ 2 + (yA - yC) ^ 2) = 2 * ((xA - (xB + xC) / 2) ^ 2 + (yA - (yB + yC) / 2) ^ 2) + 2 * ((xB - (xB + xC) / 2) ^ 2 + (yB - (yB + yC) / 2) ^ 2)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode d56cbb06), kernel_verified on the second attempt -- the first problem_version omitted problem_imports and defaulted to a Ring+NormNum-only import manifest that does not resolve the `ℝ` notation, so Lean's autoImplicit silently auto-generalized `ℝ` as an unbound implicit type variable, causing a spurious 'failed to synthesize instance of type class HSub ℝ ℝ ?m' error when `ring` tried to normalize genuine real subtraction. Re-registered a fresh problem_version with problem_imports=["Mathlib"] (same fix pattern documented in the law_of_cosines packet's notes) and it went through on the first real attempt with a plain `intro ...; ring`. Picked as this cycle's geometry target after confirming `packets/elementary/geometry/QUEUE.md`'s 'Law of cosines' backlog item had already been landed by a concurrent agent (packet law_of_cosines.v1, episode d1f31293) but the queue file was stale; the remaining backlog item, 'Law of sines', was investigated and deferred (see QUEUE.md) as genuinely L2/hard -- a faithful formalization needs an intrinsic definition of the angle at a second triangle vertex, which reintroduces the sqrt/division machinery this domain's packets deliberately avoid (see the domain's own noted `Real.sqrt` atom-matching pitfall). Apollonius's theorem was chosen instead as a same-difficulty, same-style classical triangle fact that was still missing from the domain and reuses the existing `midpoint_equidist`/`AffineAreaKit` pattern.
